EDA

Junxiu

2026-02-28

Packages

library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.1.4     ✔ readr     2.1.5
## ✔ forcats   1.0.0     ✔ stringr   1.5.1
## ✔ ggplot2   4.0.2     ✔ tibble    3.2.1
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.4     ✔ tidyr     1.3.1
## ✔ purrr     1.0.4     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(readxl)
library(janitor)
## 
## Attaching package: 'janitor'
## 
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     chisq.test, fisher.test
library(skimr)
library(naniar)
## 
## Attaching package: 'naniar'
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:skimr':
## 
##     n_complete
library(stringr)
library(genio)

File path

# phenotype
pheno_file1 <- file.path("data", "phenotype",
                         "CMS challenge Scotland 22G BE20041B sample IDs_with PCR11102022.xlsx")
pheno_file2 <- file.path("data", "phenotype",
                         "Data - CMS MOWI genetics project Scotland.xlsx")

# pedigree
ped_file <- file.path("data", "pedigree", "pedigree22G_CMSSalmoStrong.txt")

# genotype
bed_file <- file.path("data", "genotype", "2022CMSGeno_SalmoStrong.bed")
bim_file <- file.path("data", "genotype", "2022CMSGeno_SalmoStrong.bim")
fam_file <- file.path("data", "genotype", "2022CMSGeno_SalmoStrong.fam")

EDA

Phenotype

Glimpse of the data

pcr_pheno <- read_excel(pheno_file1, sheet = "Sheet2") %>%
  clean_names() %>%
  rename(
    fish_number = fish_nunber,
    test_replicate = test_replicate,
    tissue_type = tissue_type,
    reported_result = reported_result,
    cp_1 = cp_value_8,
    cp_2 = cp_value_9,
    cp_3 = cp_value_10
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    reported_result = as.factor(reported_result),
    tissue_type = as.factor(tissue_type),
    species = as.factor(species)
  )
## New names:
## • `Cp value` -> `Cp value...8`
## • `Cp value` -> `Cp value...9`
## • `Cp value` -> `Cp value...10`
head(pcr_pheno, 10)
## # A tibble: 10 × 11
##    sample_id sample_no    fish_number test_replicate species control tissue_type
##        <dbl> <chr>              <dbl>          <dbl> <fct>     <dbl> <fct>      
##  1    418442 AFH-2022-02…           1              1 Salmo …    15.6 HE         
##  2    418445 AFH-2022-02…           2              1 Salmo …    15.7 HE         
##  3    418448 AFH-2022-02…           3              1 Salmo …    15.9 HE         
##  4    418451 AFH-2022-02…           4              1 Salmo …    16.2 HE         
##  5    418454 AFH-2022-02…           5              1 Salmo …    16.6 HE         
##  6    418457 AFH-2022-02…           6              1 Salmo …    15.8 HE         
##  7    418460 AFH-2022-02…           7              1 Salmo …    16.6 HE         
##  8    418463 AFH-2022-02…           8              1 Salmo …    16.1 HE         
##  9    418466 AFH-2022-02…           9              1 Salmo …    15.9 HE         
## 10    418469 AFH-2022-02…          10              1 Salmo …    15.6 HE         
## # ℹ 4 more variables: cp_1 <dbl>, cp_2 <dbl>, cp_3 <dbl>, reported_result <fct>
histo_pheno <- read_excel(pheno_file2, sheet = "Scotland samples", skip = 1) %>%
  clean_names() %>%
  rename(
    id = id,
    atrium_area_background = area_background_2,
    atrium_area_inflamed = area_inflamed_tissue,
    atrium_area_rbc = area_red_blood_cells_4,
    atrium_area_noninflamed = area_non_inflamed_tissue_5,
    atrium_area_investigated = area_investigated_6,
    atrium_pct_inflam = percentage_inflammation,
    atrium_rel_inflam = relative_area_inflammation_8,
    spong_area_background = area_background_11,
    spong_area_inflamed = area_inflammed_tissue,
    spong_area_rbc = area_red_blood_cells_13,
    spong_area_noninflamed = area_non_inflamed_tissue_14,
    spong_area_investigated = area_investigated_15,
    spong_pct_inflam = precentage_inflammation,
    spong_rel_inflam = relative_area_inflammation_17,
    ratio_inflam = ratio_inflammation_atrium_spongiosum,
    ratio_control = ratio_controll
  )
## New names:
## • `Area background` -> `Area background...2`
## • `Area red blood cells` -> `Area red blood cells...4`
## • `Area non-inflamed tissue` -> `Area non-inflamed tissue...5`
## • `Area investigated` -> `Area investigated...6`
## • `Relative Area Inflammation` -> `Relative Area Inflammation...8`
## • `Note` -> `Note...9`
## • `` -> `...10`
## • `Area background` -> `Area background...11`
## • `Area red blood cells` -> `Area red blood cells...13`
## • `Area non-inflamed tissue` -> `Area non-inflamed tissue...14`
## • `Area investigated` -> `Area investigated...15`
## • `Relative Area Inflammation` -> `Relative Area Inflammation...17`
## • `Note` -> `Note...18`
## • `` -> `...19`
head(histo_pheno, 10)
## # A tibble: 10 × 21
##    id                atrium_area_background atrium_area_inflamed atrium_area_rbc

# Whether there are replicate of ID and fish number
pcr_pheno %>% count(sample_id) %>% filter(n > 1)
## # A tibble: 0 × 2
## # ℹ 2 variables: sample_id <dbl>, n <int>
pcr_pheno %>% count(fish_number) %>% filter(n > 1)
## # A tibble: 0 × 2
## # ℹ 2 variables: fish_number <dbl>, n <int>
histo_pheno %>% count(id) %>% filter(n > 1)
## # A tibble: 0 × 2
## # ℹ 2 variables: id <chr>, n <int>
# No!

Distribution of Phenotype

pcr_pheno %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(cp_1, cp_2, cp_3), names_to = "rep", values_to = "cp") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = cp)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  facet_wrap(~rep, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_bw()

pcr_pheno %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reported_result)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "tomato") +
  theme_bw()

pcr_pheno %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = tissue_type, fill = reported_result)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  theme_bw() +
  ylab("Proportion")

Distribution of histopathology

histo_pheno %>%
  select(atrium_pct_inflam, spong_pct_inflam, ratio_inflam) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "trait", values_to = "value") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = value)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "darkseagreen3", color = "white") +
  facet_wrap(~trait, scales = "free") +
  theme_bw()
## Warning: Removed 8 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_bin()`).

# Calculate cp mean, sd
# Calculate inflam differ and mean

pcr_pheno <- pcr_pheno %>%
  mutate(
    cp_mean = rowMeans(select(., cp_1, cp_2, cp_3), na.rm = TRUE),
    cp_sd = apply(select(., cp_1, cp_2, cp_3), 1, sd, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

histo_pheno <- histo_pheno %>%
  mutate(
    inflam_diff = atrium_pct_inflam - spong_pct_inflam,
    inflam_mean = (atrium_pct_inflam + spong_pct_inflam) / 2
  )

summary(pcr_pheno$cp_mean)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##   11.65   25.64   27.45   27.10   29.10   34.68
cat("\n")
summary(pcr_pheno$cp_sd)
##      Min.   1st Qu.    Median      Mean   3rd Qu.      Max. 
##  0.005774  0.047258  0.081854  0.204116  0.136504 21.362755
cat("\n")
summary(histo_pheno$inflam_diff)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.    NA's 
## -8.5789  0.1350  0.3739  2.1863  0.9814 86.7566       6

From the result above, the max of \(\text{cp_sd}\) value could be 21 which is a little bit large which can be think of as outlier. Double check:

pcr_pheno %>%
    arrange(desc(cp_sd)) %>% 
    select(sample_id, sample_no, cp_1, cp_2, cp_3, cp_mean, cp_sd) %>% 
    head(20)
## # A tibble: 20 × 7
##    sample_id sample_no                      cp_1  cp_2  cp_3 cp_mean  cp_sd
##        <dbl> <chr>                         <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>  <dbl>
##  1    420500 AFH-2022-0221-F687-I-HEART_MG  38.0  36.0   0      24.6 21.4  
##  2    418601 AFH-2022-0221-F54-I-HEART_MG    0    34.9   0      11.6 20.2  
##  3    419915 AFH-2022-0221-F492-I-HEART_MG  28.3  28.3  31.2    29.3  1.71 
##  4    421046 AFH-2022-0221-F869-I-HEART_MG  29.8  29.9  32.4    30.7  1.48 
##  5    418532 AFH-2022-0221-F31-I-HEART_MG   29.1  26.2  28.0    27.8  1.44 
##  6    418523 AFH-2022-0221-F28-I-HEART_MG   27.3  29.0  27.3    27.9  1.00 
##  7    420425 AFH-2022-0221-F662-I-HEART_MG  24.6  26.0  24.5    25.0  0.862
##  8    420494 AFH-2022-0221-F685-I-HEART_MG  28.1  26.9  26.8    27.3  0.688
##  9    418517 AFH-2022-0221-F26-I-HEART_MG   34.1  33.0  32.8    33.3  0.684
## 10    418502 AFH-2022-0221-F21-I-HEART_MG   32.3  33.0  33.5    32.9  0.599
## 11    418664 AFH-2022-0221-F75-I-HEART_MG   33.8  32.7  33.1    33.2  0.584
## 12    418556 AFH-2022-0221-F39-I-HEART_MG   30.2  31.1  30.4    30.6  0.496
## 13    420935 AFH-2022-0221-F832-I-HEART_MG  28.4  29.2  29.1    28.9  0.455
## 14    420896 AFH-2022-0221-F819-I-HEART_MG  32.4  32.0  31.5    32.0  0.453
## 15    420398 AFH-2022-0221-F653-I-HEART_MG  32.8  33.6  33.6    33.4  0.448
## 16    419930 AFH-2022-0221-F497-I-HEART_MG  33.7  33.1  33.9    33.6  0.440
## 17    421037 AFH-2022-0221-F866-I-HEART_MG  29.9  30.6  29.8    30.1  0.428
## 18    419882 AFH-2022-0221-F481-I-HEART_MG  29.8  29.0  29.3    29.4  0.422
## 19    419894 AFH-2022-0221-F485-I-HEART_MG  29.0  29.6  28.9    29.1  0.407
## 20    420509 AFH-2022-0221-F690-I-HEART_MG  29.8  30.2  30.6    30.2  0.405

We can see that \(\text{sample_ID}\) which 420500 and 418601 have abnormal \(\text{cp_value}\).

Two proposed methods:

  1. change 0 to ‘NA’
  2. See reported_result as categorical variable?

Now, we look at the \(\text{histo_pheno}\):

histo_pheno %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = inflam_diff)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  theme_bw()
## Warning: Removed 6 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_bin()`).

histo_pheno %>%
  arrange(desc(inflam_diff)) %>%
  select(id, atrium_pct_inflam, spong_pct_inflam, inflam_diff) %>%
  head(50)
## # A tibble: 50 × 4
##    id                      atrium_pct_inflam spong_pct_inflam inflam_diff
##    <chr>                               <dbl>            <dbl>       <dbl>
##  1 2022_0221_073 (Image 2)              89.2            2.48         86.8
##  2 2022_0221_379 (Image 2)              63.1            3.01         60.0
##  3 2022_0221_218 (Image 2)              60.1            1.10         59.0
##  4 2022_0221_035 (Image 2)              50.4            1.76         48.7
##  5 2022_0221_078 (Image 2)              41.1            1.07         40.0
##  6 2022_0221_368 (Image 2)              39.9            0.621        39.2
##  7 2022_0221_327 (Image 2)              36.6            1.13         35.5
##  8 2022_0221_121 (Image 2)              35.8            2.61         33.1
##  9 2022_0221_137 (Image 2)              31.4            0.761        30.6
## 10 2022_0221_125 (Image 2)              35.4            6.27         29.2
## # ℹ 40 more rows

We notice that there are 6 NAs, now we find them:

histo_pheno %>%
  summarise(
    n_missing_atrium = sum(is.na(atrium_pct_inflam)),
    n_missing_spong = sum(is.na(spong_pct_inflam)),
    n_missing_diff = sum(is.na(inflam_diff))
  )
## # A tibble: 1 × 3
##   n_missing_atrium n_missing_spong n_missing_diff
##              <int>           <int>          <int>
## 1                6               0              6
histo_pheno %>%
  filter(is.na(inflam_diff)) %>%
  select(id, atrium_pct_inflam, spong_pct_inflam)
## # A tibble: 6 × 3
##   id                      atrium_pct_inflam spong_pct_inflam
##   <chr>                               <dbl>            <dbl>
## 1 2022_0221_115 (Image 2)                NA           0.116 
## 2 2022_0221_130 (Image 2)                NA           0.0192
## 3 2022_0221_375 (Image 2)                NA           0.0441
## 4 2022_0221_383 (Image 2)                NA           0.0610
## 5 2022_0221_431 (Image 2)                NA           0.0260
## 6 2022_0221_491 (Image 2)                NA           0.0439

We find that 2022_0221_115, 130, 375, 383, 431, 491 are missing \(\text{atrium_pct_inflam}\) value.

Join the phenotype IDs

pcr_pheno2 <- pcr_pheno %>%
  mutate(
    batch = str_extract(sample_no, "\\d{4}-\\d{4}"),            
    fish_n = as.integer(str_extract(sample_no, "(?<=-F)\\d+")), 
    fish3  = str_pad(fish_n, width = 3, pad = "0"),            
    id_key = paste0(str_replace_all(batch, "-", "_"), "_", fish3)  
  )


histo_pheno2 <- histo_pheno %>%
  mutate(
    batch = str_extract(id, "\\d{4}_\\d{4}"),              
    fish_n = as.integer(str_extract(id, "(?<=_)\\d+(?=\\s*\\()")), 
    fish3 = str_pad(fish_n, 3, pad = "0"),
    id_key = paste0(batch, "_", fish3)
  )

pheno_joined <- pcr_pheno2 %>%
  left_join(histo_pheno2, by = "id_key")

We now get the joined form \(\text{pheno_joined}\)

head(pheno_joined, 20)
## # A tibble: 20 × 43
##    sample_id sample_no    fish_number test_replicate species control tissue_type
##        <dbl> <chr>              <dbl>          <dbl> <fct>     <dbl> <fct>      
##  1    418442 AFH-2022-02…           1              1 Salmo …    15.6 HE         
##  2    418445 AFH-2022-02…           2              1 Salmo …    15.7 HE         
##  3    418448 AFH-2022-02…           3              1 Salmo …    15.9 HE         
##  4    418451 AFH-2022-02…           4              1 Salmo …    16.2 HE         
##  5    418454 AFH-2022-02…           5              1 Salmo …    16.6 HE         
##  6    418457 AFH-2022-02…           6              1 Salmo …    15.8 HE         
##  7    418460 AFH-2022-02…           7              1 Salmo …    16.6 HE         
##  8    418463 AFH-2022-02…           8              1 Salmo …    16.1 HE         
##  9    418466 AFH-2022-02…           9              1 Salmo …    15.9 HE         
## 10    418469 AFH-2022-02…          10              1 Salmo …    15.6 HE         
## 11    418472 AFH-2022-02…          11              1 Salmo …    16.3 HE         
## 12    418475 AFH-2022-02…          12              1 Salmo …    16.4 HE         
## 13    418478 AFH-2022-02…          13              1 Salmo …    16.1 HE         
## 14    418481 AFH-2022-02…          14              1 Salmo …    15.4 HE         
## 15    418484 AFH-2022-02…          15              1 Salmo …    15.6 HE         
## 16    418487 AFH-2022-02…          16              1 Salmo …    16.5 HE         
## 17    418490 AFH-2022-02…          17              1 Salmo …    16.1 HE         
## 18    418493 AFH-2022-02…          18              1 Salmo …    15.8 HE         
## 19    418496 AFH-2022-02…          19              1 Salmo …    16.2 HE         
## 20    418499 AFH-2022-02…          20              1 Salmo …    16   HE         
## # ℹ 36 more variables: cp_1 <dbl>, cp_2 <dbl>, cp_3 <dbl>,
## #   reported_result <fct>, cp_mean <dbl>, cp_sd <dbl>, batch.x <chr>,
## #   fish_n.x <int>, fish3.x <chr>, id_key <chr>, id <chr>,
## #   atrium_area_background <dbl>, atrium_area_inflamed <dbl>,
## #   atrium_area_rbc <dbl>, atrium_area_noninflamed <dbl>,
## #   atrium_area_investigated <dbl>, atrium_pct_inflam <dbl>,
## #   atrium_rel_inflam <dbl>, note_9 <chr>, x10 <lgl>, …

Build phenotype-to-pedigree crosswalk (from Sheet1)

Sheet1 contains animal/sire/dam fields and is used as the bridge from phenotype IDs to pedigree IDs.

normalize_cms_id <- function(x) {
  x <- as.character(x)
  x[x %in% c("", "NA", "NaN", "0", "2")] <- NA_character_

  # Animal IDs in Sheet1 miss "00" after ICM
  x <- str_replace(x, "^2022IRS1ICMFR", "2022IRS1ICM00FR")

  # Parent IDs in Sheet1 miss "0000" after CBN
  x <- str_replace(x, "^2018IRS1CBN([0-9]{5}[A-Z][0-9]{2})$", "2018IRS1CBN0000\\1")
  x
}

sheet1_map <- read_excel(pheno_file1, sheet = "Sheet1") %>%
  clean_names() %>%
  transmute(
    sample_id = as.character(sample_id),
    fish_no = suppressWarnings(as.integer(fish_no)),
    genotyping_tube_id = genotyping_tube_id,
    animal = normalize_cms_id(animal),
    sire = normalize_cms_id(sire),
    dam = normalize_cms_id(dam)
  ) %>%
  distinct(sample_id, .keep_all = TRUE)
## New names:
## • `Cp value` -> `Cp value...12`
## • `Cp value` -> `Cp value...13`
## • `Cp value` -> `Cp value...14`
pheno_ped <- pheno_joined %>%
  mutate(sample_id = as.character(sample_id)) %>%
  left_join(sheet1_map, by = "sample_id")

Pedigree Information

pedigree <- read_table(ped_file, col_types = cols(
  id = col_character(),
  sire = col_character(),
  dam = col_character(),
  sibgroup = col_character(),
  YC = col_double()
)) %>%
  mutate(
    sire = na_if(sire, "0"),
    dam = na_if(dam, "0"),
    is_founder = is.na(sire) & is.na(dam),
    n_known_parents = (!is.na(sire)) + (!is.na(dam))
  )

Have a look at the pedigree structure:

head(pedigree, 20)
## # A tibble: 20 × 7

# Some summary statistics about pedigree
pedigree %>%
  summarise(
    n_animals = n(),
    n_unique_id = n_distinct(id),
    n_founders = sum(is_founder),
    n_non_founders = sum(!is_founder),
    n_two_known_parents = sum(n_known_parents == 2),
    n_one_known_parent = sum(n_known_parents == 1),
    n_no_known_parent = sum(n_known_parents == 0)
  )
## # A tibble: 1 × 7
##   n_animals n_unique_id n_founders n_non_founders n_two_known_parents
##       <int>       <int>      <int>          <int>               <int>

## # ℹ 2 more variables: n_one_known_parent <int>, n_no_known_parent <int>
pedigree %>%
  count(sibgroup, sort = TRUE) %>%
  head(15)
## # A tibble: 3 × 2
##   sibgroup     n
##   <chr>    <int>
## 1 S1CBN     2067
## 2 S1ICM      980
## 3 S1CBI        4
pedigree %>%
  count(YC, sort = TRUE)

# Read Plink files (bim/fam/bed)
plink_obj <- read_plink(tools::file_path_sans_ext(bed_file))
## Reading: data/genotype/2022CMSGeno_SalmoStrong.bim
## Reading: data/genotype/2022CMSGeno_SalmoStrong.fam
## Reading: data/genotype/2022CMSGeno_SalmoStrong.bed
geno <- plink_obj$X
bim <- plink_obj$bim
fam <- plink_obj$fam
head(fam, 10)

dim(geno)
## [1] 66541   980
dim(fam)
## [1] 980   6
dim(bim)
## [1] 66541     6
sum(fam$id %in% pedigree$id)
## [1] 980
sum(pedigree$id %in% fam$id)
## [1] 980

All genotyped samples can be matched to pedigree IDs.

fam_ped <- fam %>%
  left_join(
    pedigree %>% select(id, sire, dam, sibgroup, YC, is_founder, n_known_parents),
    by = "id"
  )

fam_ped %>%
  summarise(
    n_fam = n(),
    n_missing_in_pedigree = sum(is.na(sibgroup)),
    n_founders_in_genotyped = sum(is_founder, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_non_founders_in_genotyped = sum(!is_founder, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
## # A tibble: 1 × 4
##   n_fam n_missing_in_pedigree n_founders_in_genotyped n_non_founders_in_genoty…¹
##   <int>                 <int>                   <int>                      <int>
## 1   980                     0                       9                        971
## # ℹ abbreviated name: ¹​n_non_founders_in_genotyped
fam_ped %>%
  count(sibgroup, sort = TRUE) %>%
  head(15)
## # A tibble: 1 × 2
##   sibgroup     n
##   <chr>    <int>
## 1 S1ICM      980

Phenotype + Pedigree for Sire Model

pheno_ped %>%
  summarise(
    n_records = n(),
    n_with_animal = sum(!is.na(animal)),
    n_with_sire = sum(!is.na(sire)),
    n_with_dam = sum(!is.na(dam)),
    n_animal_in_pedigree = sum(animal %in% pedigree$id, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_sire_in_pedigree = sum(sire %in% pedigree$id, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_dam_in_pedigree = sum(dam %in% pedigree$id, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
## # A tibble: 1 × 7
##   n_records n_with_animal n_with_sire n_with_dam n_animal_in_pedigree
##       <int>         <int>       <int>      <int>                <int>
## 1       500           500         480        486                  491
## # ℹ 2 more variables: n_sire_in_pedigree <int>, n_dam_in_pedigree <int>
sire_model_dat <- pheno_ped %>%
  mutate(
    # Treat 0 as missing for Cp replicate values before averaging
    cp_1_clean = na_if(cp_1, 0),
    cp_2_clean = na_if(cp_2, 0),
    cp_3_clean = na_if(cp_3, 0),
    cp_mean_clean = rowMeans(cbind(cp_1_clean, cp_2_clean, cp_3_clean), na.rm = TRUE),
    cp_mean_clean = if_else(is.nan(cp_mean_clean), NA_real_, cp_mean_clean),

    # Keep only sires that can be mapped into pedigree
    sire = if_else(sire %in% pedigree$id, sire, NA_character_),
    tissue_type = as.factor(tissue_type),
    test_replicate = as.factor(test_replicate)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(cp_mean_clean), !is.na(sire))

sire_model_dat %>%
  summarise(
    n_records = n(),
    n_sires = n_distinct(sire),
    cp_mean_mean = mean(cp_mean_clean),
    cp_mean_sd = sd(cp_mean_clean)
  )
## # A tibble: 1 × 4
##   n_records n_sires cp_mean_mean cp_mean_sd
##       <int>   <int>        <dbl>      <dbl>
## 1       469      67         27.2       3.03
sire_model_dat %>%
  count(sire, sort = TRUE) %>%
  head(15)
## # A tibble: 15 × 2
##    sire                        n
##    <chr>                   <int>
##  1 2018IRS1CBN000011158A03    15
##  2 2018IRS1CBN000011152A07    14
##  3 2018IRS1CBN000011156G07    14
##  4 2018IRS1CBN000011153E10    12
##  5 2018IRS1CBN000011157H07    12
##  6 2018IRS1CBN000011151E02    11
##  7 2018IRS1CBN000011155D03    11
##  8 2018IRS1CBN000011151D10    10
##  9 2018IRS1CBN000011153D09    10
## 10 2018IRS1CBN000011154G07    10
## 11 2018IRS1CBN000011156A04    10
## 12 2018IRS1CBN000011156B11    10
## 13 2018IRS1CBN000011156D11    10
## 14 2018IRS1CBN000011158E01    10
## 15 2018IRS1CBN000011151D07     9
# ASReml sire model (pedigree-based relationship among sires)
# Run this block only after asreml is installed and license is active.
library(asreml)

ped_asreml <- pedigree %>%
  transmute(
    animal = id,
    sire = if_else(is.na(sire), "0", sire),
    dam = if_else(is.na(dam), "0", dam)
  )

Ainv <- ainverse(ped_asreml)

fit_sire <- asreml(
  fixed = cp_mean_clean ~ tissue_type + test_replicate,
  random = ~ vm(sire, Ainv),
  residual = ~ idv(units),
  data = sire_model_dat,
  na.method.X = "include",
  na.method.Y = "omit"
)

summary(fit_sire)$varcomp
summary(fit_sire, coef = TRUE)$coef.fixed
summary(fit_sire, coef = TRUE)$coef.random
# Simpler fallback if you want sire as IID random effect (without pedigree relationship)
fit_sire_iid <- asreml(
  fixed = cp_mean_clean ~ tissue_type + test_replicate,
  random = ~ sire,
  residual = ~ idv(units),
  data = sire_model_dat,
  na.method.X = "include",
  na.method.Y = "omit"
)

summary(fit_sire_iid)$varcomp

Genotype QC Summary

snp_call_rate <- 1 - rowMeans(is.na(geno))
sample_call_rate <- 1 - colMeans(is.na(geno))

alt_af <- rowMeans(geno, na.rm = TRUE) / 2
maf <- pmin(alt_af, 1 - alt_af)

sample_qc <- tibble(
  id = fam$id,
  call_rate = sample_call_rate,
  het_rate = colMeans(geno == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
  hom_ref_rate = colMeans(geno == 0, na.rm = TRUE),
  hom_alt_rate = colMeans(geno == 2, na.rm = TRUE)
)

snp_qc <- tibble(
  snp_id = bim$id,
  chr = bim$chr,
  pos = bim$pos,
  call_rate = snp_call_rate,
  alt_af = alt_af,
  maf = maf
)
sample_qc %>%
  summarise(
    n_samples = n(),
    mean_call_rate = mean(call_rate),
    min_call_rate = min(call_rate),
    mean_het_rate = mean(het_rate),
    sd_het_rate = sd(het_rate)
  )
## # A tibble: 1 × 5
##   n_samples mean_call_rate min_call_rate mean_het_rate sd_het_rate
##       <int>          <dbl>         <dbl>         <dbl>       <dbl>
## 1       980              1             1         0.354      0.0143
snp_qc %>%
  summarise(
    n_snps = n(),
    mean_call_rate = mean(call_rate),
    min_call_rate = min(call_rate),
    n_monomorphic = sum(maf == 0),
    n_low_maf_001 = sum(maf < 0.01),
    n_low_maf_005 = sum(maf < 0.05)
  )
## # A tibble: 1 × 6
##   n_snps mean_call_rate min_call_rate n_monomorphic n_low_maf_001 n_low_maf_005
##    <int>          <dbl>         <dbl>         <int>         <int>         <int>
## 1  66541              1             1          1209          2171          5746
sample_qc %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = het_rate)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  theme_bw() +
  xlab("Sample heterozygosity (proportion of genotype == 1)")

snp_qc %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = maf)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "darkolivegreen3", color = "white") +
  theme_bw() +
  xlab("Minor allele frequency")

snp_qc %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = call_rate)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "tomato", color = "white") +
  theme_bw() +
  xlab("SNP call rate")

Phenotype Coverage Check

pcr_ids <- pcr_pheno %>%
  mutate(fish_n = as.integer(str_extract(sample_no, "(?<=-F)\\d+"))) %>%
  distinct(fish_n) %>%
  mutate(in_pcr = TRUE)

histo_ids <- histo_pheno %>%
  mutate(fish_n = as.integer(str_extract(id, "(?<=_)\\d+(?=\\s*\\()"))) %>%
  distinct(fish_n) %>%
  mutate(in_histo = TRUE)

pheno_overlap <- full_join(pcr_ids, histo_ids, by = "fish_n") %>%
  mutate(
    in_pcr = replace_na(in_pcr, FALSE),
    in_histo = replace_na(in_histo, FALSE),
    overlap_group = case_when(
      in_pcr & in_histo ~ "Both PCR and Histo",
      in_pcr & !in_histo ~ "PCR only",
      !in_pcr & in_histo ~ "Histo only"
    )
  )

pheno_overlap %>%
  count(overlap_group)
## # A tibble: 3 × 2
##   overlap_group          n
##   <chr>              <int>
## 1 Both PCR and Histo   300
## 2 Histo only           200
## 3 PCR only             200
pheno_overlap %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = overlap_group, fill = overlap_group)) +
  geom_bar(show.legend = FALSE) +
  theme_bw() +
  ylab("Number of fish IDs") +
  xlab(NULL)

sum(as.character(pcr_pheno$sample_id) %in% fam$id)
## [1] 0
sum(pcr_pheno$sample_no %in% fam$id)
## [1] 0
sum(histo_pheno$id %in% fam$id)
## [1] 0

No direct string overlap is observed between phenotype IDs and PLINK fam$id. In this project, Sheet1 serves as the external crosswalk to connect phenotype and pedigree/genotype IDs.
